meaning: n.脚步,脚步声,一步跨出去的距离
examples: Road, in fact, is under your footstep.
I lended my ear to hear but there was no footstep.
But now, Pearl, I hear a footstep along the path, and the noise of one putting aside the branches.
Why do hospitals let doctors, orderlies and visitors slosh bacteria around with every footstep?
Into all that, Intel has made one small footstep into a very messy puddle.
